Notes: Ad-hoc, per-set UAT for the end-to-end consumer bootstrap flow: Get Started wizard / Set up new project -> uniform scaffolding (.venv + dabbler-ai-router + three engine files + docs/dabbler/start-here.md + a templated spec.md; router config Full-only) -> tell the orchestrator 'start the next session' -> it self-orients and runs in the correct tier mode (routed for Full, --no-router for Lightweight) -> closes via the shared gate. The deliverable this set verifies is human-attestable: 'a fresh operator and a cold AI orchestrator can stand up and run a Full- or Lightweight-tier repo with zero what-do-I-do-next gaps.' Items whose substance is mechanically covered are marked Passes=true with the deterministic check named in Feedback (the shared writer, the cold-start acceptance test, the drift guards, the snapshot test). Items that require a live VS Code wizard interaction, real shell scaffolding, or actually driving a second orchestrator engine are flagged 'Manual operator UAT' and left Passes=false / Result=pending for the operator to walk through on both tiers post-publish. requiresUAT was declared true in spec.md (uatScope: per-set, uatStyle: ad-hoc).

Review.Subarea: Full tier — Set up new project
Review.ItemLabel: Full scaffold writes .venv + router package + 3 engine files + start-here + templated spec + router config
Review.HumanAction: In an empty folder, run 'Dabbler: Set up new project' (dabbler.setupNewProject) and pick the Full tier. Inspect the resulting tree.
Review.Expectation: A .venv exists with dabbler-ai-router installed; CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, docs/dabbler/start-here.md, a docs/session-sets/<NNN-slug>/spec.md and session-state.json all present; ai_router/router-config.yaml IS written (Full only).
Review.Feedback:
Now, it is asking for the title of the first session set.  We don't need or want that.  I created an SVG -- docs\planning\getting-started-instructions.svg -- which lays out what I am envisioning for the Getting Started.  In the code editor window, you have the step-by-step instructions.  In the Session Set Explorer, you have the form controls that do the set up. 

When the user clicks on the Session Set Explorer action button on the left in VS Code, the system looks to see what has been completed in the setup.  If the session sets exist, it displays the session sets; otherwise, it displays the Getting Started form along with the instructions (in the code window).

As the user progresses during the set up, the UI indicates as such.  In my example, you will see that the "Build project structure" is greyed out and has a green check mark next to it.  That tells the user that the project structure has been built -- the python files are downloaded and placed into .venv; the CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and GEMINI.md files have been created; and the project folders have been created.

The setup script should validate as it goes along.  For example, if the Full tier is selected but the environment variables are not set up, the user should see a warning message just below the Build project structure button, indicating that environment variables need to be set up (but be careful here, the user may create System or User environment variables, and both should be acceptable).

If the user select Create parallel session sets where possible, the user should receive an info alert below the button that the AI orchestration will use git worktrees to manage parallel session sets and that these worktrees will be merged back into the main branch when the session sets complete.

After you have reviewed the SVG document and the above, let's discuss.  Yes, this probably requires a session set to build and test this functionality, but it will be worth it!  Trust me.

Review.Subarea: Lightweight tier — Set up new project
Review.ItemLabel: Lightweight scaffold is IDENTICAL to Full except: no router config, tier: lightweight
Review.HumanAction: In a second empty folder, run 'Dabbler: Set up new project' and pick the Lightweight tier. Inspect the tree and diff it against the Full one.
Review.Expectation: Same .venv + dabbler-ai-router + three engine files + start-here.md + templated spec + session-state.json as Full. The ONLY differences: no ai_router/router-config.yaml (and no budget.yaml) is written, and the spec's config block carries tier: lightweight. Python is NOT skipped; the .venv and package install are present (Lightweight is router-off, not Python-off).

Review.ItemLabel: Wizard states Python is required for BOTH tiers
Review.HumanAction: Open the Get Started wizard (the webview). Read the prerequisites / tier selection copy for both Full and Lightweight.
Review.Expectation: The wizard tells the operator they need Python + a .venv + dabbler-ai-router on BOTH tiers. It does NOT claim Lightweight skips Python / the venv / the router package. The tier model is linked to the SSoT rather than restated.

Review.ItemLabel: Wizard ends with an explicit 'tell your orchestrator: start the next session' closure
Review.HumanAction: Complete the wizard for a new project. Observe the final step / closing message.
Review.Expectation: The wizard ends by telling the operator they are ready and to tell their AI orchestrator 'start the next session' — no dead-end, no 'what do I do now?' gap. The closure points at docs/dabbler/start-here.md.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT — live wizard flow. The closure target (start-here.md) and its self-orientation procedure are mechanically present (cold-start acceptance asserts the engine files point to start-here.md and start-here states the active-set rule + start_session/close_session).

Review.ItemLabel: Every engine file routes a cold orchestrator to docs/dabbler/start-here.md
Review.HumanAction: Open CLAUDE.md (and AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md) in the scaffolded repo. Confirm each immediately directs you to the cold-start doc.
Review.Expectation: All three engine files share a body that says 'start every session here' and link docs/dabbler/start-here.md; they do not restate the lifecycle inline.
Review.Feedback: Mechanically verified — test_cold_start_acceptance.py asserts all three engine files contain 'docs/dabbler/start-here.md' for both tiers. Operator spot-check optional.

Review.ItemLabel: start-here.md states the one machine-checkable active-set rule verbatim
Review.Expectation: It states the verbatim rule: the active set is the single docs/session-sets/<NNN-slug>/ whose session-state.json has status: in-progress; at most one; if none, the lowest-NNN not-started set; complete/cancelled are skipped. Followed by Steps 2-5 (read tier, register, do work, close via gate).
Review.Feedback: Mechanically verified — test_cold_start_acceptance.py asserts start-here.md contains 'The active session set is the single directory', 'exactly one active set', 'lowest', and both start_session/close_session. The CI drift guard enforces 'at most one in-progress set' repo-wide (test_drift_guard one-active-set).

Review.ItemLabel: A Full repo, told only 'start the next session', runs ROUTED
Review.HumanAction: In the Full scaffolded repo, tell your AI orchestrator 'start the next session'. Observe that start_session runs without --no-router and routed calls are made.
Review.Expectation: The orchestrator resolves the single active set, reads tier: full, and runs in routed mode (metered cross-provider verification available at session end).
Review.Feedback: Mechanically verified — test_cold_start_acceptance.py[full] asserts runtime_mode.resolve_no_router_mode(...) is False for the Full golden, then registers + closes through the shared gate. Live drive of a real engine is the operator portion.

Review.ItemLabel: A Lightweight repo, told only 'start the next session', runs --no-router (zero metered calls)
Review.HumanAction: In the Lightweight scaffolded repo, tell your AI orchestrator 'start the next session'. Confirm no metered API calls are made and the session still registers/closes.
Review.Expectation: The orchestrator resolves the active set, reads tier: lightweight, and runs in --no-router mode — zero metered API calls — while start_session / close_session and the state file still work exactly as on Full.
Review.Feedback: Mechanically verified — test_cold_start_acceptance.py[lightweight] asserts resolve_no_router_mode(...) is True and close_session --no-router succeeds through the soft gate. Live drive on a real engine is the operator portion.

Review.ItemLabel: Generated spec.md carries tier + verificationMode, NNN- slug, schemaVersion 4 — never schemaVersion 2
Review.HumanAction: Open the generated docs/session-sets/<slug>/spec.md and session-state.json in a scaffolded repo (either tier).
Review.Expectation: spec.md's Session Set Configuration block has tier and verificationMode; the folder slug is NNN- prefixed; session-state.json is schemaVersion 4. No bare-slug folder and no schemaVersion: 2 anywhere.
Review.Feedback: Mechanically verified — consumerBootstrap.test.ts asserts the rendered spec carries tier + verificationMode + totalSessions and the state is schemaVersion 4 / 'never emits schemaVersion 2'; isCanonicalSlug rejects bare slugs. sessionGenPrompt.test.ts pins the NNN- prompt contract.

Review.ItemLabel: 'Generate Session-Set Prompt' emits the canonical shape, not the old schemaVersion: 2 / bare-slug shape
Review.HumanAction: Run 'Dabbler: Generate Session-Set Prompt' and read the produced prompt / template guidance.
Review.Expectation: The prompt instructs an NNN- slug, schemaVersion 4, tier + verificationMode; it never shows the legacy schemaVersion: 2 or a bare slug. Worked examples use 001-... shapes.
Review.Feedback: Mechanically verified — sessionGenPrompt.test.ts asserts the rendered prompt routes through the shared writer and uses NNN-/schemaVersion-4/tier shapes. The generated artifact equals the snapshot golden (coldStartSnapshot.test.ts).

Review.ItemLabel: The stale 'Lightweight = no Python / no venv / docs-only' framing cannot reappear in any live doc
Review.HumanAction: Optionally: add the phrase 'Lightweight means no Python' to any live doc (e.g. README.md) and run 'python ai_router/scripts/drift_guard.py'.
Review.Expectation: The drift guard exits non-zero and names the file:line; CI goes red. Removing the phrase makes it pass. Historical docs/session-sets and docs/proposals are out of scope; the SSoT's banned-phrase catalogue is allow-region-exempt.
Review.Feedback: Mechanically verified — test_drift_guard.py covers flagged/clean/allow-region/excluded-subtree/HTML cases and asserts the real repo is clean; the guard runs as a dedicated CI job and inside pytest.

Review.ItemLabel: Generated stubs + start-here.md stay in lock-step with the canonical template bundle
Review.HumanAction: Optionally: edit a file under docs/templates/consumer-bootstrap/ without recompiling, then run the drift guard and the TS test suite.
Review.Expectation: The dist-in-sync guard flags the un-recopied dist bundle, and the coldStartSnapshot.test.ts golden comparison fails until UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 regenerates the goldens — so silent template drift is impossible.
Review.Feedback: Mechanically verified — coldStartSnapshot.test.ts compares the live render to committed goldens for both tiers; drift_guard check_dist_bundle_in_sync compares committed dist to the canonical source (byte-equal, CRLF-normalized).

Review.ItemLabel: README, adoption-bootstrap, wizard, and engine files point to the tier-model SSoT instead of paraphrasing it
Review.HumanAction: Skim README.md, docs/adoption-bootstrap.md, the wizard copy, and a scaffolded repo's engine files for the tier explanation.
Review.Expectation: Each links docs/concepts/tier-model.md (or its GitHub blob URL) and uses the 'router-off, not Python-off' framing; none restates the full model or uses the stale framing.
Review.Feedback: Mechanically backed — the drift guard's real-repo-green run proves the stale framing is absent from all live docs and the engine-file body links the SSoT (Set 058 S1 deliverable). Operator readability spot-check optional.

Review.ItemLabel: The extension still activates and existing commands work after the bootstrap rework
Review.HumanAction: Open any workspace with the 0.28.0 build; confirm the extension activates and the Session Sets view renders.
Review.Expectation: Clean activation; the Session Sets view and existing commands work; no manifest errors from the new/changed scaffolding commands.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT — live activation. The TS unit suite (incl. the new snapshot test) and the Python suite (incl. cold-start acceptance + drift guards) pass; tsc is clean. Confirm activation in a real VS Code host.
